message:
perf(redis): add RESP completeness pre-check to eliminate O(N^2) parse-allocate-fail cycles

The RESP parser's decode_at() recursively allocates RespValue nodes as it
traverses the buffer. When the buffer contains a partial (fragmented)
message, the parser would allocate all nodes up to the fragment boundary,
discover the buffer is incomplete, discard everything, and retry on the
next recv — classic Schlemiel the Painter's algorithm on large arrays.

New check_complete() function does a zero-allocation depth-first scan of
the buffer to verify the entire RESP structure is present before calling
decode_at(). It validates type bytes, CRLF boundaries, bulk string
lengths, array counts, and nesting depth against RedisProtocolLimits
without creating any RespValue nodes. If incomplete, returns Ok(None)
immediately so the caller can wait for more data without wasting
allocations.

The existing decode_at() path is unchanged and only runs after
check_complete() confirms the full structure is buffered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
